mnemonics.title: FCFS Fun Phrase
mnemonics.content: First Come First Served – 'Fred Came, First Served' helps remember FCFS processes requests in the order they arrive.

mnemonics.title: SSTF Snappy Sentence
mnemonics.content: Shortest Seek Time First – 'Sally Seeks The Fastest' reminds you to always pick the request closest to the current head position.

mnemonics.title: SCAN Elevator Chant
mnemonics.content: SCAN (Elevator) – 'Scanner Goes Back and Forth, Like an Elevator' signals the arm moves to one end, then reverses direction.

mnemonics.title: C‑SCAN Carousel Cycle
mnemonics.content: Circular SCAN – 'Circular Scanner, Carousel Around' shows the head sweeps to one end, jumps back to the start, and repeats.

mnemonics.title: LOOK Light‑weight Scan
mnemonics.content: LOOK – 'Last Request Only, Omit End' reminds you that it behaves like SCAN but stops at the farthest request in each direction.

mnemonics.title: C‑LOOK Circular LOOK
mnemonics.content: Circular LOOK – 'Circular LOOK, Carousel Back' indicates a circular service that jumps to the first request after reaching the last.

acronyms.usage: Use when you need a simple, fair policy with no starvation; processes disk requests in exact arrival order.
Use when the primary goal is to minimize the seek time; may cause starvation for distant requests.
Use when you want balanced service; the head moves in one direction to the end, then reverses, serving all requests along the way.
Use when you need uniform wait times; the head sweeps in one direction, jumps back to the start, and repeats the sweep.
Similar to SCAN but stops at the last request in each direction, avoiding unnecessary travel to the disk edge.
Use for a circular service without reaching the extremes; the head moves to the last request then jumps to the first request.

visualTips.visualization: Picture a line of people waiting at a ticket counter; the first person in line gets served first.
Imagine a dartboard where you always aim at the bullseye closest to where your previous dart landed.
Think of an elevator that travels up to the top floor, then switches direction and comes down, stopping at every floor.
Imagine a carousel ride where horses move around in a circle, then reset to the starting point after each full rotation.
Visualize an elevator that only travels between the highest and lowest occupied floors, skipping empty floors.
Think of a rotating conveyor belt that picks items in one direction, then loops back to the start without covering empty space.

analogies.analogy: A single‑lane road where cars enter in order and exit in the same order.
A grocery checkout where the next customer chosen is the one with the fewest items.
An elevator in a building that stops at every floor on its way up, then goes down stopping at each floor.
A rotating turnstile that gives each person a ride one by one around a circle, then resets to the beginning.
An elevator that only travels between the highest and lowest floor that have people waiting, skipping empty floors.
A circular bus route that picks up passengers at the last stop, then jumps back to the first stop to start a new loop, skipping empty stops.
